title: campanula pusilla var. hochstetteri
intro: Campanula pusilla var. hochstetteri, also known as Alpine Bellflower, is a rare and short-lived perennial plant species native to the European Alps. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation. Alpine Bellflower can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its herbal benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
